# Online Dispute Resolution: A Game Changer

![Fox Mandal logo](//files.lexology.com/images/lexology/firm/new/2c19e5e5-2fe6-4f71-9260-8253592e59b3/W350/20250305120122.png)
![Saurabh Bindal](//files.lexology.com/images/lexology/authorv2/squaretop/9f178799-3b8c-4902-9bec-ff3d16f724d4/W100/H100/20220829080046/photo.jpg "Lexology author: Saurabh Bindal")

As online commerce has grown, so has the necessity for a dispute resolution system to handle any disagreements that may arise. In 1996, researchers at the University of Massachusetts and the University of Maryland took the first steps towards developing ODR programmes. eBay’s online mediation model for buyer-seller disagreements was launched in the year 1999. To help its users work out their differences, eBay implemented a system of online mediation. In just two weeks, this project was able to settle 200 disagreements. In 2010, eBay’s online mediation process resolved more than 60 million conflicts, allowing the company to reach more equitable outcomes for all parties involved. The global success of these relatively small platforms has attracted the interest of governments throughout the world. In 2004, New York County was the first in the nation to use ODR. After seeing the success of ODR systems like Brazil’s Consumidor.gov and Europe’s European Online Disputes Resolution Platform, governments around the world began adopting similar systems.

In June 2020, Niti Aayog, in India, brought together key stakeholders, including senior judges of the Supreme Court, secretaries from key government ministries, and leaders of the industry, for advancing ODR in India. Thereafter, on June 10, 2021, a handbook was released containing rules, policies, and expectations from the system. India is in grave need of a system like ODR because the Indian judicial system is under tremendous pressure with over 4.7 million cases pending across different levels of the judiciary. Due to this, there is a huge backlog of cases that are pending for the past 30 years.

ODR is time and cost-effective, and this is a very important factor in delayed justice. The economic burden associated with dispute resolution can hinder a person’s access to justice. Anyone with the help of ODR can do the arbitration from anywhere, and ODR also has the potential to reduce legal costs. The system of ODR is quicker at giving awards and resolving issues than ADR. Since it’s conducted online, the synchronisation of schedules is also done with much ease. It also eliminates the bias made by human judgement as it is much more secure and strict and the proceedings are easily available, and any higher authority can review the whole process.

For any system to grow, the governance framework must encourage the growth of innovation both within the government and the private sector. Even though ODR is still in its development stages, it has already started to show great potential by reducing the load on overburdened courts and resolving matters more effectively. ODR is the future of dispute resolution. We should accept it rather than oppose it, as it delivers efficiency and is cost-effective. Overall, ODR has the potential to transform the Indian judicial system by being a more efficient and accessible system of dispute resolution. With the right support, ODR can become an integral part of India’s legal ecosystem and contribute to the country’s growth and development.

--- SOURCE 2: Buying and Selling Tools: Dispute Resolution Overview - eBay ---
URL: https://pages.ebay.com/services/buyandsell/disputeres.html

SUMMARY:
All eBay buyers and sellers can use this online dispute resolution service. It's free to file a complaint. SquareTrade will contact and encourage the other party to respond to your case. You can then try and settle your dispute through SquareTrade's free Web-based process and patent-pending technology.

FULL CONTENT:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Dispute Resolution Overview** [Square Trade Logo](https://www.squaretrade.com) | |
| * [What is online dispute resolution?](#what)* [What are the benefits and costs of this service?](#service)* [How can I start the dispute resolution process?](#how)  What is online dispute resolution?  Online dispute resolution is a new, unbiased method that can help you resolve disputes that may arise involving eBay transactions. [SquareTrade](https://www.squaretrade.com), eBay’s preferred dispute resolution provider, offers two services: a free web-based forum which allows users to attempt to resolve their differences on their own or if necessary, the use of a professional mediator.   What are the benefits and costs of this service?  All eBay buyers and sellers can use this online dispute resolution service. It’s free to file a complaint. SquareTrade will contact and encourage the other party to respond to your case.   You can then try and settle your dispute through SquareTrade’s free Web-based process and patent-pending technology. A significant number of complaints are directly resolved in this way.   Because we believe this service helps make eBay a better place to trade, you can request the assistance of a professional mediator for $15 (eBay will subsidize the rest of the cost).   Benefits  In addition to turning a negative episode into a positive one, using SquareTrade can help:  * Resolve misunderstandings fairly* Provide a neutral go-between for buyers and sellers;* Reduce pre-mature negative feedback* Generate trust in the eBay community  How can I start the dispute resolution process?  To start the process, [file a complaint](https://www.squaretrade.com/user_registration/default.cfm). You will be asked to complete these steps via an online form:  * Create a SquareTrade User ID and password* Enter complaint details.  SquareTrade will send a notification email to the other party who can then respond to you. Your complaint and the other party’s response will appear in a secure area on the SquareTrade Web site. Only you, the other party and the mediator (if you choose to involve one) will be granted access. Disputes are often successfully settled with this independent resolution method.   If you’d like, you can also use a SquareTrade mediator for $15 to guide you through the Web-based process. The mediator works to understand both points of view and to help develop a fair, agreeable settlement. If a resolution can’t be reached, the mediator will recommend a solution based on principles of fairness and good conduct.   This process generally takes 10 days. Learn more about SquareTrade or file a complaint by clicking [here](https://www.squaretrade.com). |

--- SOURCE 3: Online Dispute Resolution: Companies Implementing ODR ---
URL: https://libraryguides.missouri.edu/c.php?g=557240&p=3832247

SUMMARY:
eBay handles over 60 million disputes a year. eBay's ODR system handles problems like "item not received," "item not as described," and "unpaid

FULL CONTENT:
![Banner](//d2jv02qf7xgjwx.cloudfront.net/sites/37/groups/12/banner/LG_Banner.png)

# Online Dispute Resolution: Companies Implementing ODR

## Introduction: Companies Implementing ODR

Many companies have turned to using ODR as a method of dispute resolution. eBay, PayPal, and ICANN are the front runners in ODR, and thus will be highlighted in this research guide. It is important to note companies using ODR are not doing this to resolve an exceptionally large number of disputes. Rather, the goal is to maximize the number of successful transactions, and resolving disputes is essential to increasing that volume.

ODR is key for businesses because it is estimated 3-5% of e-commerce transactions ends in a dispute. For sites without a feedback or reputation system that users can consult before making a purchase, the percentage is even greater. ODR is a neutral process that provides quick decisions to both the buyer and seller.

## eBay

**History of eBay ODR**

eBay began ODR in the late 1990s when an experienced human mediator used email to interact with the disputants using the same strategies with which he engaged disputants offline (e.g., assisted storytelling and joint problem solving). eBay's ODR system features a purposeful design that challenges the boundaries of existing ADR approaches, which incorporates various types of computer mediated communications (CMC). eBay ODR can be used by both buyers and sellers.

**eBay ODR Tiered System**

There are five main steps to eBay's ODR system outlined below:

1. Go to Resolution Center

2. Resolution Center asks a series of questions to diagnose the problem and ensure the complaint is within eBay’s coverage for a Money Back Guarantee and that the purchaser used the “pay now” option and asserted the complaint within 30 days after the actual or estimated delivery date.

## PayPal

**History of PayPal ODR**

In 2010, eBay and PayPal conducted a study that was not intended to measure satisfaction in the traditional manner, by surveying disputants before and after participating in a dispute resolution process. Rather, it would compare the actual behavior of participants before and after the process, something it could easily measure with data they routinely collected. eBay did not look at what users said but at their actions as buyers or sellers after participating in ODR.

**PayPal ODR Tiered System**

## Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers (ICANN)

**History of ICANN**

The domain naming system was invented in 1984 but grew rapidly starting in the mid-1990s. In 1990, there were 8,000 domain names but by 2000 there were over a million. Today, there are over 290 million. ICANN has handled over 50,000 disputes between owners of a domain name and holders of a trademark that is identical or similar to the domain name. ICANN requires that organizations that provide arbitrators publish the decisions. Almost all of the cases heard by an arbitrator are from either the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) or the National Arbitration Forum (NAF). Statistics show that both organizations rule in favor of trademark holders approximately 85% of the time. Because of this, a small percentage of domain names are currently being challenged because those behind the website have a good idea of how the arbitrators will rule. ICANN's work in the domain name arbitration process has been very successful. ICANN is much less expensive than going to court, and decisions are usually made in fewer than 40 days.

**ICANN ODR Tiered System**

To invoke the UDRP, a trademark owner should either (a) file a complaint in a court of proper jurisdiction against the domain name holder (or where appropriate an in-rem action concerning the domain name) or (b) in cases of abusive registration, submit a complaint to an approved dispute-resolution service provider.

# How eBay Resolution Center Can Help Resolve Seller Problems?

![How eBay Resolution Center Can Help Resolve Seller Problems?](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/eBay-resolution-center-fb-1024x538.jpg)

Running short of time? Get PDF of the blog in your mail.

![](https://cedcommerce.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/icons8-download-from-cloud-48.png)
![](https://cedcommerce.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/icons8-download-from-cloud-48.png)
![Processing](https://cedcommerce.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Circle_loading_2.gif "Processing")
![](https://domr-zc1.maillist-manage.in/images/videoclose.png)

Learning how to use the eBay Resolution Center, also referred to as the eBay Dispute Center, is vital for every eBay seller. Most eBay purchases go smoothly, but you may come across a buyer issue at some point. The eBay Resolution Centre is a place where you can solve these problems. It is the place where you may request eBay’s assistance if you are unable to resolve a dispute with your buyer on your own.

To contact, visit the eBay Seller Resolution Center and press ‘Report a problem’ or press the help button in case you need to open a dispute. The eBay eBay Resolution Centre makes sure your selling journey on eBay is safe and easy. We’ll break down its functions and benefits, ensuring your online transactions are smooth and secure.

## What is the eBay Resolution Center?

You can report issues that come up after a sale to the eBay Resolution Centre for eBay. This is where you can also track the status of your grievance.

[![Integrate Shopify](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/Integrate-eBay-with-Shopify.jpg "Integrate eBay with Shopify")](https://apps.shopify.com/ebay-integration?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=akshay&utm_campaign=trivedi "Integrate eBay with Shopify")

![Integrate Shopify](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/Integrate-eBay-with-Shopify.jpg "Integrate eBay with Shopify")

## How does the eBay Resolution Center work?

As an eBay seller, you can take the following steps if you have a problem with an unpaid transaction or the buyer’s cancellation of the sale:

#### Step 1: Start the Request

The [eBay Dispute Center](https://resolutioncenter.ebay.com/ "eBay Resolution Center") is located in the [Help and Contact](https://www.ebay.com/help/home "eBay Help") section, which can be visited on the [eBay homepage](https://www.ebay.com/ "eBay Home"). There you can file a request on the unpaid transaction or the sale was cancelled.

#### Step 2: Use eBay Resolution Tools

Once you’ve opened a request, the eBay Resolution Centre for eBay provides tools for communicating with your buyers and responding to any questions they may have. Be sure to respond to any buyer queries within three business days.

#### Step 3: Stay updated

Keep yourself updated with any messages from your buyers and respond promptly. If your issue is not resolved, you can request eBay’s assistance by asking them to step in and help you.

## eBay Seller Resolution is Genuinely Concerned

eBay has all the details secured on its databases. It does monitor fraudulent buyers and ensures that such identities are restricted. If a buyer has a history of unpaid sales, eBay will impose certain restrictions on them. This action is meant to protect sellers like you from being scammed.

The eBay Dispute Center also assists in the cancellation of sales. If you have accepted an order that you cannot send due to issues on your part, the Resolution Center will help you. An order canceled from the eBay Dispute Centre will save you from the risk of [underperformance of the seller.](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/steps-to-improve-performance-on-ebay-marketplace/ "Improving performance on eBay")

## How to open a case on eBay

You, as an eBay seller sometimes have to take action and also open a case against their buyer. These cases may occur when a buyer doesn’t pay for the commodity or if there is a need to cancel an order upon acceptance. Thus, as a seller, you protect yourself and guarantee that eBay remains a reliable and safe platform for all.

Nevertheless, one should keep in mind when opening a case only in a dire situation. When not meeting stipulated conditions, it is imperative to give the buyer a chance to cooperate and develop a solution. However, after reviewing the case, the eBay trading platform may cancel the transaction as a result of which action may be taken against the buyer.

### #1. Cancellation of a transaction

At times you may have to cancel a transaction because the good is out of stock, or when you do not want to sell it. In such a case, visit the eBay Resolution Center and follow the instructions. Pick the reason you are canceling the transaction, for instance: “item out of stock” or “buyer requested to cancel”. Moreover, it is essential to mention that you should only cancel a transaction in necessary cases, such as when the good is out of stock or, you are unable to finish the sale.

The case should only be opened when necessary and as a last resort. Before opening a case, try communicating with the buyer and finding a solution together.

### #2. Unpaid Item Assistant

If you are selling multiple items and find it challenging to go through this process often, you can activate the Unpaid Item Assistant. After opting for the Unpaid Item Assistant, your grievances will automatically open and close. This will help you save time. Also, this will ensure you don’t miss receiving the final value fee for each case raised.

Remember that there is a time limit to close an unpaid item case. The time limit is 32 days. If you don’t close the unpaid item case within this time limit, eBay will close the open case. But if eBay does so, you will not be paid anything as the final value fee of the item.

[![Install integration for ebay app](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Install-integration-for-ebay-app-CTA.jpg "Install integration for ebay app")](https://apps.shopify.com/ebay-integration?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=akshay&utm_campaign=trivedi "Install integration for ebay app")

![Install integration for ebay app](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Install-integration-for-ebay-app-CTA.jpg "Install integration for ebay app")

## How to use the eBay Resolution Centre?

If a buyer has yet to pay you for the sale, you can open up the case in the eBay Resolution Center and ask eBay to step in and mediate. But before reporting the issue, you should try your best to contact the buyer and get things sorted between you.

### #1. Raising a Complaint with eBay Resolution Centre

If you have contacted the buyer and the buyer has not paid you even after two days of making the sale, you can raise an unpaid item grievance in the eBay Dispute Centre. Let’s see how to do so.

The marketplace will guide you further with the next steps.

If you have sold an item on eBay but cannot deliver the order and want to cancel the sale, the Resolution Center is again for you. If you don’t cancel the sale through the eBay Resolution Centre, this will negatively impact your seller’s performance on eBay.

[![How to raise a complaint with eBay resolution center](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/How-to-raise-a-complaint-with-eBay-resolution-center.jpg "How to raise a complaint with eBay resolution center")](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/How-to-raise-a-complaint-with-eBay-resolution-center.jpg "How to raise a complaint with eBay resolution center")

![How to raise a complaint with eBay resolution center](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/How-to-raise-a-complaint-with-eBay-resolution-center.jpg "How to raise a complaint with eBay resolution center")

### #2. Closing a Complaint

Now, if the buyer pays through your selected payment method on eBay after the mediation of the Resolution Center, the eBay open case will be automatically closed for you. Now you don’t need to do anything at your end.

#### #2.1 Closing a Complaint after getting a resolution

But, if the buyer pays with any other method, you must go to the “eBay Seller Hub”/”My eBay” and mark the item as delivered. Afterward, go to the eBay Resolution Centre and mark the case closed. Let’s see how you can do it.

#### #2.2 Closing a Complaint when the eBay open case is unresolved

Now let’s tell you about another possibility you must be ready for while selling on eBay. There are chances that the buyer might not pay you even several days after you raised a complaint on the eBay Resolution Centre. But there is a time limit to take action on this possibility. The time limit is five days.

If, even after five days of opening the complaint on the eBay Resolution Center, the buyer hasn’t paid you, you can close the complaint by selecting different options. Here’s what you can do.

The eBay eBay Resolution Center will record this item on the buyer’s account. And then, the marketplace will credit the final value fee back to you. Now you can relist the item.

[![How to close a complaint with eBay resolution center](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/How-to-close-a-complaint-with-eBay-resolution-center.jpg "How to close a complaint with eBay resolution center")](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/How-to-close-a-complaint-with-eBay-resolution-center.jpg "How to close a complaint with eBay resolution center")

![How to close a complaint with eBay resolution center](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/How-to-close-a-complaint-with-eBay-resolution-center.jpg "How to close a complaint with eBay resolution center")

## FAQs: eBay eBay Resolution Centre

### 1. How long does the resolution process typically take?

The timeline can change depending on the complexity of the case and the time frame in which sellers and buyers respond. Sellers only have 3 business days to respond.

In case eBay is asked to acquire the case, the platform strives to analyze cases, and return to users with their decision as fast as possible, which may take as little as 48 hours if enough information is provided. Nonetheless, in most situations, the end-to-end process will take from several days to some weeks.

### 2. What happens if the buyer disagrees with the seller’s claims?

If after 3 working days, both parties can’t come to a mutual agreement, eBay can be invited to intervene in the case. eBay will look through the submitted information and give the result.

When eBay decides in favor of the buyer, you may be given a defect or it can also negatively influence your performance rating. Sellers can also report a buyer who breached eBay policy via the Seller Hub, the eBay Resolution Centre, or the Sold Items List​​.

### 3. Are there any fees associated with using the eBay Resolution Center?

Opening a case in the eBay Resolution Center (eBay Dispute Center) per se is not charged. Yet, there are possible financial consequences for sellers which may occur if the situation with the dispute evolves, and eBay takes the side of the buyer.

Thus, the seller would pay off not only a certain sum, which is the refund, but also depending on the outcome of the dispute, he/she might be charged with a defect​​.

### 4. How can sellers protect themselves from fraudulent buyers?

A seller is obliged to write an honest description of the product. Also, you should behave professionally and describe their refusal to return the product.

eBay gives you an opportunity to report the problematic buyers, who, in their opinion, violated the policy. Another opportunity is to ask for a review, feedback, or ban the buyer.

### 5. Can sellers appeal eBay’s decision if they disagree with the outcome of a resolution?

Sellers can contest the decision on eBay regarding photographic evidence and an additional 30 days after the completion of the case. eBay notifies the seller about the revision of evidence and will always decide within 48 hours after an appeal. Hence, Since the appeal was created for the seller, it is vital to present essential accurate claims and evidence.

## Conclusion

The eBay Resolution Center can open cases, communicate with your buyers, and resolve your issues. Utilize it smartly to get the best results and save final value fees in events when your buyers fail to pay for orders. Do you have more queries regarding the eBay Resolution Centre or the Dispute Center? Feel free to contact our marketplace experts at CedCommerce.

[Planning to sell on eBay](https://cedcommerce.com/marketplace-integration/ebay-integration/ "Sell on eBay with CedCommerce") and need clarification on [how to do so?](https://cedcommerce.com/blog/ebay-selling/ "Sell on eBay") We’d be more than glad to guide you through this. [Schedule a meeting](https://calendly.com/scale-business-with-cedcommerce/marketplace-integration?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=akshay&utm_campaign=trivedi "Schedule a Call") for any selling-related assistance.

enriched_points.expanded_content: eBay launched its ODR system in 1999, effectively utilizing online mediation to resolve disputes, setting a precedent for e-commerce platforms worldwide.
By 2010, eBay's ODR successfully resolved over 60 million conflicts, enhancing fairness in transactions and fostering trust within its user community.
eBay’s ODR includes a user-friendly interface, real-time mediation, and a tiered resolution process, leading to quicker resolutions and reduced legal costs for users.
